description: Canonical structured log schema for frankenlibc unit/conformance/e2e/perf/release workflows. All log lines must be JSONL (one JSON object per line) conforming to this schema.

rules: New optional fields may be added within schema_version=2 without breaking existing emitters/consumers.
Required fields must not change without a schema_version bump.
Enum vocabularies should only expand (never rename) within a schema_version.
Emitters may add extra fields not listed here inside `details` (preferred) or as top-level fields during migrations; validators should be tolerant unless a gate explicitly opts into strictness.

emitter_checklist: Emit one JSON object per line (JSONL), no trailing commas.
Always include: timestamp, trace_id, level, event.
trace_id must be stable for the run and follow <bead_id>::<run_id>::<seq>.
Prefer setting bead_id explicitly even though it can be derived from trace_id.
Set stream and gate for joinable aggregation across workflows.
When applicable, set mode (strict|hardened), api_family, and symbol.

For new ambition-wide evidence rows, emit event=ambition_evidence and include bead_id, scenario_id, runtime_mode, replacement_level, api_family, symbol, oracle_kind, expected, actual, errno, decision_path, healing_action, latency_ns, source_commit, target_dir, failure_signature, and non-empty artifact_refs.
On membrane-related events, set decision and profile; on repairs set healing_action.
On runtime_decision events (event=runtime_decision), always include controller_id, decision_action, and risk_inputs object.
On failures, set outcome=fail and include artifact_refs pointing to diffs/backtraces/reports.
Put large/variable payloads in details (object) rather than inventing many ad-hoc top-level keys.

trace_id.description: Correlation ID linking log entries across a single test/e2e run

event.description: Machine-readable event name (e.g., test_start, validation_pass, abi_call)

bead_id.description: Bead that owns this test/verification run

gate.description: Pipeline step / gate name (e.g., ci, e2e_suite, perf_gate, closure_gate)

scenario_id.description: Scenario identifier for manifest-driven runs (e.g., fault injection, shadow run)

mode.description: Legacy TSM runtime mode under test

runtime_mode.description: Canonical runtime mode for ambition evidence rows; when both mode and runtime_mode are present they must match

replacement_level.description: Replacement-readiness level proved by this row: L0 interpose, L1 hardened interpose, L2 standalone limited, L3 standalone broad

api_family.description: API family (e.g., malloc, string, stdio, pthread, signal)

symbol.description: Specific ABI symbol being tested (e.g., memcpy, malloc, strlen)

oracle_kind.description: Oracle used to decide expected behavior for this row

expected.description: Expected value or structured expectation for row-level comparison

actual.description: Actual observed value or structured observation for row-level comparison

decision_path.description: Explainable control-flow / policy path taken for this event

span_id.description: Optional span id for multi-component traces under one trace_id

decision_id.description: Canonical decision identifier for cross-artifact correlation

policy_id.description: Canonical policy identifier for cross-artifact correlation

evidence_seqno.description: Canonical evidence sequence number for ledger/replay correlation

profile.description: Membrane validation profile selected by runtime policy

healing_action.description: Healing action applied (hardened mode) when applicable

controller_id.description: Runtime controller identity responsible for decision routing

decision_action.description: Explainability action label emitted by decision controller

risk_inputs.description: Explainability risk/context inputs used to select decision action

exit_code.description: Exit code for external processes/scripts when relevant

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock duration for a higher-level gate step (milliseconds)

source_commit.description: Git commit or source revision used to produce this evidence row

target_dir.description: Build or run target directory, typically an isolated CARGO_TARGET_DIR for rch-backed gates

failure_signature.description: Deterministic failure classifier; successful ambition rows use none when this field is required

artifact_refs.description: Paths to related artifacts (snapshots, diffs, backtraces)

details.description: Free-form additional context (test inputs, expected vs actual, etc.)

artifact_index_schema.description: Format for artifact index files linking logs to verification artifacts

run_id.description: Unique run identifier matching trace_id run component

properties.kind: log | snapshot | golden | diff | backtrace | report
properties.sha256: SHA-256 hex digest for integrity verification

join_keys.description: Optional correlation keys tying an artifact to log/evidence rows
